lines.target: It is often said that the English are conservative.
lines.base: Se dice frecuentemente que los ingleses son conservadores.

lines.target: I prefer to hear it said that they are traditionalist.
lines.base: Prefiero que se diga (oír decir) que son tradicionalistas.

lines.target: There are many traditions and customs in Britain and some may appear strange to foreigners.
lines.base: Hay muchas tradiciones y costumbres en Gran Bretaña y algunas pueden parecer extrañas a los extranjeros.

lines.target: For example, on October the thirty-first, one can see children making masks from pumpkins
lines.base: Por ejemplo, en octubre, el día treinta y uno, podemos ver a los niños haciendo máscaras con calabazas

lines.target: and putting candles inside to frighten witches.
lines.base: y poniendo velas dentro para asustar a las brujas.

lines.target: This is called Halloween and is the day before All Saints’ Day.
lines.base: Se llama Halloween y es la víspera (día antes) de Todos los Santos.

lines.target: Just before Easter, on Good Friday, you can buy Hot Cross Buns.
lines.base: Poco antes de la Pascua, el Viernes Santo, se pueden comprar los Hot Cross Buns.

lines.target: These are delicious spicy cakes, with a cross on them, to remind us of the Crucifixion.
lines.base: Son deliciosos pasteles de especias, con una cruz encima (sobre ellos) para recordarnos de la Crucifixión.

lines.target: The day after Christmas is called Boxing Day.
lines.base: El día siguiente de Navidad se llama Boxing Day.

lines.target: This is because householders used to give little presents or "boxes" to the tradesmen who had served them.
lines.base: Esto se debe a que los cabezas de familia acostumbraban a dar pequeños regalos o "cajas" a los comerciantes que les habían servido.

lines.target: Nowadays, it is usual to give money.
lines.base: Hoy día se acostumbra a dar dinero.

lines.target: Perhaps the most spectacular tradition is "Bonfire Night" or "Guy Fawkes Night".
lines.base: Quizá la tradición más espectacular es "la noche de las hogueras", o Guy Fawkes Night.

lines.target: This takes place on the fifth of November and celebrates the arrest, in sixteen-oh-five ,
lines.base: Tiene lugar el cinco de noviembre y festeja el arresto, en 1605,

lines.target: of Guy Fawkes, who attempted to blow up the Houses of Parliament.
lines.base: de Guy Fawkes, quien intentó hacer saltar por los aires el Parlamento.

lines.target: Today, people celebrate by lighting bonfires, setting off fireworks and burning effigies called "guys".
lines.base: Hoy día, la gente lo festeja encendiendo hogueras, lanzando fuegos artificiales y quemando efigies llamadas guys.

gap_candidates.target: It is often said that the English are conservative.
I prefer to hear it said that they are traditionalist.
For example, on October the thirty-first, one can see children making masks from pumpkins
and putting candles inside to frighten witches.
This is called Halloween and is the day before All Saints’ Day.
Just before Easter, on Good Friday, you can buy Hot Cross Buns.
The day after Christmas is called Boxing Day.
Nowadays, it is usual to give money.
Perhaps the most spectacular tradition is "Bonfire Night" or "Guy Fawkes Night".
of Guy Fawkes, who attempted to blow up the Houses of Parliament.
Today, people celebrate by lighting bonfires, setting off fireworks and burning effigies called "guys".
